title: Cua the dog walks alone
summary: Cua is a small dog who walks himself to the fish shop and home every morning — and once saved his owner's life by sitting at the door.
body:
On Le Loi Street there is a small dog named Cua. Cua means 'crab' in Vietnamese, because he walks sideways when he is excited.

Cua lives with two old people, Mr Tien and his wife. They are seventy-five. They cannot walk far. So in the morning, Mrs Tien opens the door, and Cua goes out alone.

Cua knows the route. He walks down Le Loi Street, turns right at the post office, walks past the school, says hello to the school guard, and turns left at the fish shop. The fish-shop owner gives him a piece of fish. Cua eats it on the corner. Then he walks home.

The whole trip takes twenty-five minutes. Every day, the same.

The neighbours laughed at first. They said, 'Dogs cannot walk alone in the city!' But Cua came home every day. After one year, the neighbours stopped laughing.

Last summer, Mrs Tien was ill. She could not open the door. Cua sat at the door for one hour. Then Cua started to cry.

A neighbour heard. She knocked. Mr Tien opened the door. The neighbour called the doctor.

Mrs Tien is fine now. The neighbour says, 'Cua saved her. He is not a dog. He is a small alarm.'

Cua does not know this. He still walks the same route. He still gets his fish.

annotations.meaningVi: sang ngang, chéo qua một bên
chuông báo, tín hiệu cảnh báo
đi ngang qua (mà không dừng lại)
ngừng cười (stop + V-ing = ngừng làm gì)

grammarNotes.title: 'Can' and 'cannot' for ability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'can + V' để nói có khả năng, và 'cannot + V' để nói không có khả năng. Trong bài: 'They cannot walk far.' / 'Dogs cannot walk alone in the city!' / 'She could not open the door.' (Quá khứ của 'cannot' là 'could not'.)
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'can + verb' for ability and 'cannot + verb' for inability. From the story: 'They cannot walk far.' / 'Dogs cannot walk alone in the city!' / 'She could not open the door.' (Past form: 'could not'.)

blanks.explanation: He walks sideways when he is excited — like a crab.
Mr and Mrs Tien are seventy-five.
Mrs Tien opens the door every morning to let Cua out.
He turns right at the post office.
The fish-shop owner gives him a piece of fish.
The whole trip takes twenty-five minutes.
The neighbours laughed at first — they did not believe a dog could walk alone.
Mrs Tien was ill — and could not open the door.
The neighbour knocked at the door when she heard Cua crying.
She called the doctor — for Mrs Tien.
'He is a small alarm' — his crying alerted the neighbour.

translationVi:
Ở đường Lê Lợi có một con chó nhỏ tên Cua. Cua nghĩa là 'con cua' trong tiếng Việt, vì nó đi nghiêng sang một bên khi nó hào hứng.

Cua sống cùng hai người già, ông Tiến và vợ ông. Họ bảy mươi lăm tuổi. Họ không đi xa được. Thế nên mỗi sáng, bà Tiến mở cửa, và Cua đi ra một mình.

Cua biết đường. Nó đi dọc đường Lê Lợi, rẽ phải ở bưu điện, đi qua trường học, chào bác bảo vệ trường, rồi rẽ trái ở cửa hàng cá. Bà chủ cửa hàng cá cho nó một miếng cá. Cua ăn ở góc phố. Rồi nó về nhà.

Cả chuyến đi mất hai mươi lăm phút. Ngày nào cũng vậy.

Lúc đầu hàng xóm cười. Họ nói: 'Chó làm gì biết đi một mình trong thành phố!' Nhưng ngày nào Cua cũng về. Sau một năm, hàng xóm thôi cười.

Mùa hè năm ngoái, bà Tiến bị ốm. Bà không mở được cửa. Cua ngồi ở cửa một tiếng. Rồi Cua bắt đầu kêu.

Một người hàng xóm nghe thấy. Cô gõ cửa. Ông Tiến mở cửa. Cô gọi bác sĩ.

Bà Tiến giờ khỏe. Người hàng xóm nói: 'Cua đã cứu bà ấy. Nó không phải con chó. Nó là một cái chuông báo nhỏ.'

Cua không biết điều đó. Nó vẫn đi cùng một con đường. Vẫn lấy miếng cá của mình.

questions.prompt: Why is the dog called Cua?
options: Because he walks sideways when excited — like a crab
Because his owner is a crab seller
Because he was found near the sea
questions.explanation: 'Because he walks sideways when he is excited.'

questions.prompt: How old are Mr and Mrs Tien?
Why does Cua go out alone?
options: Mr and Mrs Tien cannot walk far, so they let him go alone
questions.explanation: 'They cannot walk far. So in the morning, Mrs Tien opens the door, and Cua goes out alone.'

options: Down Le Loi Street, right at the post office, past the school, left at the fish shop
questions.explanation: Each turn is named in paragraph 3.

questions.prompt: What does Cua get from the fish-shop owner?
questions.explanation: 'The fish-shop owner gives him a piece of fish.'

questions.prompt: How long does the whole trip take?
What did the neighbours do at first?
options: Laughed and said dogs cannot walk alone in the city
Mrs Tien was ill and could not open the door; Cua sat at the door and started to cry
A neighbour, who knocked and called the doctor
questions.explanation: 'A neighbour heard. She knocked. Mr Tien opened the door. The neighbour called the doctor.'

questions.prompt: What does the neighbour say about Cua now?
options: He is a very large dog
He is not a dog — he is a small alarm; he saved her
questions.explanation: 'He is not a dog. He is a small alarm.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The neighbour calls Cua a 'small alarm', but Cua does not know that. Think of someone or something in your life that is quietly important to you without knowing it. What would change if it knew?
